member.name: T:Bonsai.Mixer.CreateMixerContext
member.summary: Represents an operator that creates a new mixer stream context used to control simultaneous playback of multiple audio buffers.

member.name: P:Bonsai.Mixer.CreateMixerContext.HostApi
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the host API that should be used to implement low-level audio processing.
member.remarks: If neither nor are specified, the default output device will be selected.

member.name: P:Bonsai.Mixer.CreateMixerContext.DeviceName
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the device that should be used to implement low-level audio processing.
member.remarks: If neither nor are specified, the default output device will be selected.

member.name: P:Bonsai.Mixer.CreateMixerContext.SampleRate
member.summary: Gets or sets the desired sample rate of the output stream, in samples per second.

member.name: P:Bonsai.Mixer.CreateMixerContext.SuggestedLatency
member.summary: Gets or sets the desired output latency, in seconds.
member.remarks: If not specified, the default low output latency value for the selected device will be used. Setting this value low may not guarantee workable playback. Testing and benchmarking are always recommended to assess the minimum required latency.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Mixer.CreateMixerContext.Generate
member.summary: Generates an observable sequence that initializes and returns a new mixer stream context object which can be used to control simultaneous playback of multiple audio buffers.
member.exception: The output sequence will emit an error if the specified target device or host API cannot be found.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Mixer.MixerStreamContext
member.summary: Represents an audio mixer which allows asynchronous queueing of multiple buffers for simultaneous playback over a single PortAudio output stream.

member.name: P:Bonsai.Mixer.MixerStreamContext.SampleRate
member.summary: Gets the sample rate of the stream, in samples per second.
member.remarks: In cases where the hardware sample rate is inaccurate and PortAudio is aware of it, the value of this field may be different from the sample rate parameter used to initialize the mixer context.

member.name: P:Bonsai.Mixer.MixerStreamContext.OutputLatency
member.summary: Gets the output latency of the stream, in seconds.
member.remarks: This value provides the most accurate estimate of output latency available to the implementation. It may differ significantly from the suggested latency value.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Mixer.MixerStreamContext.QueueBuffer(OpenCV.Net.Mat)
member.summary: Adds a new buffer to the mixer stream context work queue.
member.remarks: At any one moment when the mixer stream context is playing, the data streamed to PortAudio will be the sum of all queued buffers being played.
member.param: A multi-dimensional array containing the sample data, where each row vector represents data from an individual channel to be mixed into the mixer output stream.
member.exception: The number of rows in the buffer does not match the number of channels in the stream.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Mixer.MixerStreamContext.Start
member.summary: Initiates mixer audio processing.
member.remarks: Any pre-queued buffers will begin playing simultaneously when the stream callback starts requesting data.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Mixer.MixerStreamContext.Stop
member.summary: Stops mixer audio processing.
member.remarks: The stream callback will stop requesting data.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Mixer.MixerStreamContext.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes the mixer context and closes the audio stream.
member.remarks: If the audio stream is active, it discards any pending buffers.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Mixer.QueueBuffer
member.summary: Represents an operator that adds audio buffers in the sequence to the work queue of a mixer stream context.

member.name: P:Bonsai.Mixer.QueueBuffer.Mixer
member.summary: Gets or sets the mixer stream context on which to queue the audio buffer.
member.remarks: This property must be mapped dynamically.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Mixer.QueueBuffer.Process(System.IObservable{OpenCV.Net.Mat})
member.summary: Adds audio buffers in an observable sequence to the work queue of the specified mixer stream context.
member.param: A sequence of objects representing audio buffers to be queued in the mixer stream context.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the sequence but where there is an additional side effect of queueing the audio buffers in the sequence into the specified mixer stream context.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Mixer.StartMixer
member.summary: Represents an operator that initiates audio processing on every mixer stream context in the sequence.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Mixer.StartMixer.Process(System.IObservable{Bonsai.Mixer.MixerStreamContext})
member.summary: Initiates audio processing on every mixer stream context in an observable sequence.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the sequence but where there is an additional side effect of initiating audio processing on every mixer stream context in the sequence.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Mixer.StopMixer
member.summary: Represents an operator that stops audio processing on every mixer stream context in the sequence.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Mixer.StopMixer.Process(System.IObservable{Bonsai.Mixer.MixerStreamContext})
member.summary: Stops audio processing on every mixer stream context in an observable sequence.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the sequence but where there is an additional side effect of stopping audio processing on every mixer stream context in the sequence.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Mixer.WorkQueue`1
member.summary: Multiple producer / single consumer queue of work items.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements stored in the work queue.
member.remarks:
Provides support for multiple producers to asynchronously enqueue items to be processed by a single consumer. A double buffering strategy is used to ensure the consumer remains lock-free. Importantly, the buffer swap happens when the work on the consumer queue is entirely done.
It is possible producers are still in the process of adding an item during the swap, but there is no critical work done immediately after, so this is acceptable. Processing of all active work items is done on a different buffer altogether and the newly swapped out queue will only be accessed at the start of the next cycle.
The assumption is that by the time we are done with everything and need the next items we will be done with processing a single Add call.
